description: A systematic way of doing something; an ordered approach to achieving ends. 'Norae' names the pattern of practice—the how that makes possible the what.
sound_symbolism: The word orders gently. 'No' settles with grounded structure, pattern established. 'Ra' radiates with systematic spread, method extending. Then 'e' opens to application.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo norae' (methods). The pattern that guides 'teru' (process). Related to 'shimalo' (discipline) as tool to practice.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Skillful means—methods adapted to context and capacity, not rigid rules.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Traditional methods—time-tested ways passed down through generations.
